## Message Type: Space Weather Notification - M5.1 Flare
##
## Message Issue Date: 2025-12-27T02:17:47Z
## Message ID: 20251227-AL-002
##
## Disclaimer: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center is the United States Government official source for space weather forecasts. This "Experimental Research Information" consists of preliminary NASA research products and should be interpreted and used accordingly.


## Summary:

Significant flare detected by GOES.

Flare start time: 2025-12-27T01:38Z. 

Flare peak time: 2025-12-27T01:50Z.

Flare intensity: M5.1 class.

Source region: S09E72 (Active Region 14323) (based on SDO imagery).

Increased energetic proton fluxes possible. Updates on this event will be provided when available.

Activity ID: 2025-12-27T01:38:00-FLR-001.

## Notes:

"},{"index":2,"messageType":"RBE","messageID":"20251226-AL-002","messageURL":"https://kauai.ccmc.gsfc.nasa.gov/DONKI/view/Alert/43599/1","messageIssueTime":"2025-12-26T19:52:00.000Z","messageBody":"## Community Coordinated Modeling Center Database Of Notifications, Knowledge, Information ( CCMC DONKI )
## Message Type: Space Weather Notification - Continued Radiation Belt Enhancement
##
## Message Issue Date: 2025-12-26T19:52:44Z
## Message ID: 20251226-AL-002
##
## Disclaimer: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center is the United States Government official source for space weather forecasts. This "Experimental Research Information" consists of preliminary NASA research products and should be interpreted and used accordingly.


## Summary:

GOES "greater than 2.0 MeV" integral electron flux remains elevated, recently crossing the threshold of 1000 pfu at 2025-12-26T07:30Z.

The elevated energetic electron flux levels observed since 2025-12-23T08:55Z (see notification 20251224-AL-001) are caused by a coronal hole high speed stream starting on 2025-12-21, reaching a maximum sustained speeds of approximately 800km/s. The solar wind speed observed at L1 is currently around 490 km/s.

NASA spacecraft at GEO, MEO and other orbits passing through or in the vicinity of the Earth's outer radiation belt can be impacted.

Activity ID: 2025-12-23T08:55:00-RBE-001. 

## Notes: 


Significantly elevated energetic electron fluxes in the Earth's outer radiation belt. GOES "greater than 2.0 MeV" integral electron flux is above 1000 pfu starting at 2025-12-23T08:55Z.
 
The elevated energetic electron flux levels are caused by a coronal hole high speed stream starting on 2025-12-21, reaching a maximum speed of approximately 800km/s. Solar wind speeds are currently around 700km/s.
 
NASA spacecraft at GEO, MEO and other orbits passing through or in the vicinity of the Earth's outer radiation belt can be impacted.
 
Activity ID: 2025-12-23T08:55:00-RBE-001.
 
## Notes:
 
This ongoing event is preceded by a radiation belt enhancement first observed at 2025-12-16T18:15Z which is associated with the arrival of a coronal hole high speed stream starting on 2025-12-12 (see notifications 20251216-AL-001, 20251216-AL-002, 20251218-AL-001, 20251220-AL-001, and 20251222-AL-001).





"},{"index":3,"messageType":"CME","messageID":"20251226-AL-001","messageURL":"https://kauai.ccmc.gsfc.nasa.gov/DONKI/view/Alert/43597/1","messageIssueTime":"2025-12-26T18:28:00.000Z","messageBody":"## Community Coordinated Modeling Center Database Of Notifications, Knowledge, Information ( CCMC DONKI )
## Message Type: Space Weather Notification - CME (Parker Solar Probe, Mars, Psyche)
##
## Message Issue Date: 2025-12-26T18:28:48Z
## Message ID: 20251226-AL-001
##
## Disclaimer: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center is the United States Government official source for space weather forecasts. This "Experimental Research Information" consists of preliminary NASA research products and should be interpreted and used accordingly.


## Summary:

C-type CME detected by STEREO A / GOES / SOHO. 

Start time of the event: 2025-12-26T14:23Z.

Estimated speed: ~742 km/s.

Estimated opening half-angle: 25 deg.

Direction (lon./lat.): -161/-19 in Heliocentric Earth Equatorial coordinates.

Activity ID: 2025-12-26T14:23:00-CME-001

Based on preliminary analysis by the Moon to Mars Space Weather Analysis Office and heliospheric modeling carried out at NASA Community Coordinated Modeling Center, it is estimated that the CME may impact Parker Solar Probe, Mars (minor impact), and Psyche (minor impact).  The leading edge of the CME will reach Parker Solar Probe at 2025-12-27T13:06Z, Mars at 2025-12-30T02:00Z, and Psyche at 2025-12-30T18:00Z (plus minus 7 hours). 

Links to the movies of the modeled event (includes CME: 2025-12-26T14:23:00-CME-001):

http://iswa.gsfc.nasa.gov/downloads/20251226_182700_2.0_anim.tim-den.gif
http://iswa.gsfc.nasa.gov/downloads/20251226_182700_2.0_anim.tim-vel.gif
http://iswa.gsfc.nasa.gov/downloads/20251226_182700_2.0_ENLIL_CONE_timeline.gif
http://iswa.gsfc.nasa.gov/downloads/20251226_182700_2.0_ENLIL_CONE_PSP_timeline.gif
http://iswa.gsfc.nasa.gov/downloads/20251226_182700_2.0_ENLIL_CONE_Mars_timeline.gif
http://iswa.gsfc.nasa.gov/downloads/20251226_182700_2.0_ENLIL_CONE_Psyche_timeline.gif


## Notes: 


SCORE CME typification system:
S-type: CMEs with speeds less than 500 km/s
C-type: Common 500-999 km/s
O-type: Occasional 1000-1999 km/s
R-type: Rare 2000-2999 km/s
ER-type: Extremely Rare >3000 km/s


"},{"index":4,"messageType":"RBE","messageID":"20251224-AL-001","messageURL":"https://kauai.ccmc.gsfc.nasa.gov/DONKI/view/Alert/43586/1","messageIssueTime":"2025-12-24T19:49:00.000Z","messageBody":"## Community Coordinated Modeling Center Database Of Notifications, Knowledge, Information ( CCMC DONKI )
## Message Type: Space Weather Notification - Continued Radiation Belt Enhancement
##
## Message Issue Date: 2025-12-24T19:49:16Z
## Message ID: 20251224-AL-001
##
## Disclaimer: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center is the United States Government official source for space weather forecasts. This "Experimental Research Information" consists of preliminary NASA research products and should be interpreted and used accordingly.
 
 
## Summary:
 
Significantly elevated energetic electron fluxes in the Earth's outer radiation belt. GOES "greater than 2.0 MeV" integral electron flux is above 1000 pfu starting at 2025-12-23T08:55Z.
 
The elevated energetic electron flux levels are caused by a coronal hole high speed stream starting on 2025-12-21, reaching a maximum speed of approximately 800km/s. Solar wind speeds are currently around 700km/s.
 
NASA spacecraft at GEO, MEO and other orbits passing through or in the vicinity of the Earth's outer radiation belt can be impacted.
 
Activity ID: 2025-12-23T08:55:00-RBE-001.
 
## Notes:
 
This ongoing event is preceded by a radiation belt enhancement first observed at 2025-12-16T18:15Z which is associated with the arrival of a coronal hole high speed stream starting on 2025-12-12 (see notifications 20251216-AL-001, 20251216-AL-002, 20251218-AL-001, 20251220-AL-001, and 20251222-AL-001).
 
"},{"index":5,"messageType":"Report","messageID":"20251224-7D-001","messageURL":"https://kauai.ccmc.gsfc.nasa.gov/DONKI/view/Alert/43582/1","messageIssueTime":"2025-12-24T16:35:00.000Z","messageBody":"## Community Coordinated Modeling Center Database Of Notifications, Knowledge, Information (CCMC DONKI)
## Message Type: Weekly Space Weather Summary Report for December 17, 2025 - December 23, 2025
##
## Message Issue Date: 2025-12-24T16:35:29Z
## Report Coverage Begin Date: 2025-12-17T00:00Z
## Report Coverage End Date: 2025-12-23T23:59Z
## Message ID: 20251224-7D-001
##
## Disclaimer: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center is the United States Government official source for space weather forecasts. This "Experimental Research Information" consists of preliminary NASA research products and should be interpreted and used accordingly.


## Summary:

Solar activity reached moderate levels during this reporting period with 3 M-class flares, 1 O-type CME, and 6 C-type CMEs (see details below).

Based on preliminary analysis by the Moon to Mars Space Weather Analysis Office and heliospheric modeling carried out at NASA Community Coordinated Modeling Center, it was estimated that the following CMEs were predicted to impact the listed locations:

CME Starting time  | Predicted Impacts (plus minus 7 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
2025-12-19T01:25Z   BepiColombo at 2025-12-19T23:47Z, Juice at 2025-12-21T23:00Z (glancing blow) (see notification 20251219-AL-001).

Geomagnetic activity was at minor levels with Kp index (a measure of geomagnetic activity, ranging 0-9) <=5.00 for the reporting period. The highest value of Kp = 5.00 occurred during the synoptic periods of 2025-12-21T21:00Z to 2025-12-22T00:00Z and 2025-12-22T12:00Z to 2025-12-22T15:00Z. This enhancement in geomagnetic activity was associated with the arrival of a coronal hole high speed stream detected at L1 by DSCOVR and ACE on 2025-12-21.

The >2.0 MeV energetic electron flux levels remained elevated above the threshold of 1000 pfu due to a threshold crossing from the previous reporting period starting at 2025-12-16T18:15Z (see notifications 20251216-AL-001, 20251216-AL-002, 20251218-AL-001, 20251220-AL-001, 20251222-AL-001). This elevation of energetic electron flux levels was likely associated with the arrival of a coronal hole high speed stream detected at L1 by DSCOVR and ACE on 2025-12-12. The energetic electron flux levels remained elevated through the end of the reporting period.

The energetic proton flux of >10 MeV protons detected at GOES and the 13-100 MeV protons at STEREO A were all at background levels for the entire reporting period. 

Space Weather Impact:
Space weather impacts on NASA spacecraft are expected to have been moderate this reporting period due to the elevated energetic electron flux in the Earth's outer radiation belt described above.

##Events:
Flares (M-class and above)
Date         Start     Stop      Peak      Class   Location         
2025-12-19  15:35:00  16:08:00  15:58:00   M1.0   S15W90 ( 14307 ) 
2025-12-20  07:36:00  08:06:00  07:53:00   M1.0   N19E57 ( 14315 ) 
2025-12-21  18:12:00  18:36:00  18:27:00   M1.3   S10E32 ( 14316 ) 

CMEs (>500 km/s):

Earth directed:
NONE

Non-Earth directed (POS = Plane Of Sky):
Starting time        Speed (km/s)   Type   Direction (LON/LAT in HEEQ)   Half-Angle width (degrees)  Detecting spacecraft (real-time)  
2025-12-17T00:30Z     ~873            C         NONE(POS)/-9                10                          GOES                              
2025-12-17T18:15Z     ~743            C              -148/53                25                          GOES, STEREO A                    
2025-12-18T11:12Z     ~507            C              -119/45                19                          SOHO, STEREO A, GOES              
2025-12-18T11:24Z     ~575            C              -169/70                11                          SOHO, STEREO A, GOES              
2025-12-19T01:25Z     ~833            C               126/-8                11                          SOHO, STEREO A, GOES              
2025-12-20T16:48Z     ~1207           O         NONE(POS)/13                10                          SOHO                              
2025-12-20T22:36Z     ~838            C         NONE(POS)/7                 26                          SOHO, GOES                        


## Space Weather Outlook:
## Outlook Coverage Begin Date: 2025-12-24T00:00Z 
## Outlook Coverage End Date:  2025-12-30T23:59Z

Solar activity is expected to vary between low and moderate levels during the outlook period. There are currently seven numbered Active Regions on the Earth-facing disk. Active Region 14317 (N11E44) has produced C-class flaring during the reporting period and may continue to produce similar levels of flaring activity during the outlook period.

Geomagnetic activity is expected to vary between low and moderate levels during the outlook period. The energetic electron flux levels at GOES mentioned above exceeded the threshold of 1000 pfu starting at 2025-12-16T18:15Z (see notifications 20251216-AL-001, 20251216-AL-002, 20251218-AL-001, 20251220-AL-001, 20251222-AL-001) and may continue to be elevated during the outlook period due to the arrival of a coronal hole high speed stream detected at L1 by DSCOVR and ACE on 2025-12-21. 


## Notes: 
SCORE CME typification system:
S-type: CMEs with speeds less than 500 km/s
C-type: Common 500-999 km/s
O-type: Occasional 1000-1999 km/s
R-type: Rare 2000-2999 km/s
ER-type: Extremely Rare >3000 km/s


"},{"index":6,"messageType":"RBE","messageID":"20251222-AL-001","messageURL":"https://kauai.ccmc.gsfc.nasa.gov/DONKI/view/Alert/43569/1","messageIssueTime":"2025-12-22T19:48:00.000Z","messageBody":"## Community Coordinated Modeling Center Database Of Notifications, Knowledge, Information ( CCMC DONKI )
## Message Type: Space Weather Notification - Continued Radiation Belt Enhancement
##
## Message Issue Date: 2025-12-22T19:48:46Z
## Message ID: 20251222-AL-001
##
## Disclaimer: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center is the United States Government official source for space weather forecasts. This "Experimental Research Information" consists of preliminary NASA research products and should be interpreted and used accordingly.


## Summary:

Significantly elevated energetic electron fluxes in the Earth's outer radiation belt. GOES "greater than 2.0 MeV" integral electron flux remains elevated, recently crossing the threshold of 1000 pfu at 2025-12-22T14:40Z.

The elevated energetic electron flux levels observed since 2025-12-16T18:15Z are caused by the arrival of coronal hole high speed stream starting on 2025-12-12, reaching sustained speeds of approximately 715 km/s (see notifications 20251216-AL-001, 20251216-AL-002, 20251218-AL-001, and 20251220-AL-001). A subsequent coronal hole high speed stream starting on 2025-12-22 has further enhanced the electron fluxes. The solar wind speed observed at L1 is currently around 800 km/s.

NASA spacecraft at GEO, MEO and other orbits passing through or in the vicinity of the Earth's outer radiation belt can be impacted.

Activity ID: 2025-12-16T18:15:00-RBE-001. 

## Notes:

The solar wind speed observed at L1 is currently around 420 km/s.

NASA spacecraft at GEO, MEO and other orbits passing through or in the vicinity of the Earth's outer radiation belt can be impacted.
